title: Agent Identity & Environment Fingerprinting
description: Use cases for establishing stable device identity and fingerprinting the runtime environment for agent tracking and integrity checks.

actors.name: GEP Agent
actors.description: The running Evolver/GEP agent process. Initiates all identity and fingerprinting operations.

actors.name: PrivacyClient
actors.description: Uses crypto primitives to encrypt/decrypt blobs for privacy-preserving hub compute.

actors.name: PolicyCheck
actors.description: Consumes captureEnvFingerprint to enforce runtime execution policies.

actors.name: File System
actors.description: Provides and stores persistent device UUID (device_id file) and evolver package.json for version/client info.

actors.name: OS / Node.js Runtime
actors.description: Provides process.version, process.platform, process.arch, os.release(), os.hostname(), process.cwd().

actors.name: Node.js Crypto
actors.description: Underlying cryptographic primitives: randomBytes, createHash (sha256), createHmac, createCipheriv/createDecipheriv (AES-256-GCM).

use_cases.name: Get Stable Device ID
use_cases.description: Read or create a persistent UUID anchoring this machine's identity. Reads from disk cache; if absent, computes from system attributes and persists. Returns UUID string.

use_cases.name: Detect Container Environment
use_cases.description: Detect whether the agent is running inside a container (Docker/cgroup) by checking /.dockerenv, cgroup, and hostname patterns. Returns boolean.

use_cases.name: Capture Environment Snapshot
use_cases.description: Assemble a full runtime environment snapshot: calls getDeviceId(), isContainer(), reads evolver package.json for version/client, hashes hostname and cwd (12-char sha256 slice), captures node_version/platform/arch/os_release/region/captured_at.

use_cases.name: Generate Environment Fingerprint Key
use_cases.description: Produce a compact 16-char hex key from an EnvFingerprintObject by joining key identity fields (device_id, node_version, platform, arch, os_release, evolver_version, client_version) with '|' and computing a SHA-256 HMAC. Returns 'unknown' for null/non-object input.

use_cases.name: Check Same Environment Class
use_cases.description: Compare two environment fingerprints to determine if they represent the same execution class. Computes envFingerprintKey for each and compares. Note: cwd differences do not affect class equality; device_id differences do.

use_cases.name: Canonicalize Asset Data
use_cases.description: Produce a deterministic JSON-like string from any value: null/undefined → 'null', booleans → 'true'/'false', non-finite numbers → 'null', strings → JSON-quoted, arrays → '[...]' preserving order, objects → '{...}' with sorted keys recursively.

use_cases.name: Compute Asset Content Hash
use_cases.description: Compute a stable 'sha256:' + 64-hex-char content hash of an asset object. Canonicalizes the object (excluding the asset_id field itself) before hashing. Used to assign stable IDs to Gene/Capsule/EvolutionEvent assets.

use_cases.name: Verify Asset Integrity
use_cases.description: Verify that an asset object's asset_id field matches the computed content hash. Re-runs computeAssetId and compares to obj.asset_id. Detects tampering or corruption.

use_cases.name: Generate Encryption Key
use_cases.description: Generate 32 cryptographically random bytes for use as an AES-256-GCM symmetric key.

use_cases.name: Encrypt Data
use_cases.function: encrypt(plaintext: Buffer|string, key: Buffer): {ciphertext, iv, authTag}
use_cases.description: Encrypt plaintext using AES-256-GCM. Validates key is exactly 32 bytes, generates random 12-byte IV, produces ciphertext + 16-byte authentication tag. Returns structured object with ciphertext, iv, and authTag Buffers.

use_cases.name: Decrypt Data
use_cases.function: decrypt(ciphertext: Buffer, key: Buffer, iv: Buffer, authTag: Buffer): Buffer
use_cases.description: Decrypt AES-256-GCM ciphertext. Validates key length (32 bytes), creates decipher with IV, sets authTag for authenticated decryption, returns plaintext Buffer.

use_cases.name: Pack Crypto Payload
use_cases.description: Serialize AES-GCM parts into a single binary Buffer: Buffer.concat([iv(12B), authTag(16B), ciphertext(variable)]). Used before transport/storage.

use_cases.name: Unpack Crypto Payload
use_cases.description: Deserialize a packed crypto Buffer back into {iv: buf[0..12], authTag: buf[12..28], ciphertext: buf[28..]}. Throws on undersized input.

include_relationships: UC5 (isSameEnvClass) <<include>> UC4 (envFingerprintKey) [applied to both fp1 and fp2]
actor_to_usecase: GEP Agent → UC1, UC2, UC3, UC4, UC5, UC6, UC7, UC8, UC9, UC10, UC11, UC12, UC13
PrivacyClient → UC9 (generateKey), UC10 (encrypt), UC11 (decrypt), UC12 (pack), UC13 (unpack)
File System ← UC1 (reads/writes device UUID file), UC3 (reads package.json for evolver_version/client)
OS / Node.js Runtime ← UC3 (process.version, platform, arch, os.release, hostname, cwd)
Node.js Crypto ← UC4 (createHmac sha256), UC6 (createHash sha256), UC7 (createHash sha256), UC9 (randomBytes), UC10 (createCipheriv aes-256-gcm), UC11 (createDecipheriv aes-256-gcm)

envFingerprint.js: envFingerprintKey(fp): string (16-char hex, 'unknown' on bad input)

key_dependencies: envFingerprint.js imports getDeviceId, isContainer from deviceId.js
contentHash.js uses Node.js crypto (createHash sha256) and crypto.createHmac
crypto.js uses Node.js crypto (randomBytes, createCipheriv, createDecipheriv) exclusively
deviceId.js uses Node.js crypto, os, fs, path
PrivacyClient (privacyClient.js) imports generateKey, encrypt, decrypt, pack, unpack from crypto.js
important_notes: All four modules (deviceId.js, envFingerprint.js, contentHash.js, crypto.js) are heavily obfuscated with hex-encoded string literals and an RC4-like string rotation cipher — a software protection pattern.
deviceId.js provides the identity anchor: a persistent UUID that survives restarts, backed by a file on disk.
hostname and cwd in the EnvFingerprintObject are hashed to 12-char sha256 slices to avoid leaking local path information.
isSameEnvClass ignores cwd differences — it keys on device_id, node_version, platform, arch, os_release, and evolver/client version.
computeAssetId excludes the asset_id field itself from hashing (prevents circular dependency when the field is pre-populated).
crypto.js uses AES-256-GCM — authenticated encryption that combines confidentiality and integrity via the authTag.
The packed binary layout is deterministic: [IV(12)] + [authTag(16)] + [ciphertext(variable)].
source_files_analyzed: ir_postprocessed.json (function entries for all four modules)
